pr.title: cdc: support human-readable byte sizes in kafka_sink_config MaxBytes
pr.body:
Previously, the `Flush.MaxBytes` field in `kafka_sink_config` only
accepted raw integer values. Attempting to use human-readable strings
like `"1MiB"` resulted in a JSON unmarshal error because the field
was typed as `int32`.

This was inconsistent with `Flush.Frequency`, which already supports
human-readable duration strings via the `jsonDuration` custom type.

This commit introduces a `jsonByteSize` type (analogous to
`jsonDuration`) that can unmarshal both raw integer values and
human-readable byte size strings (e.g. `"1MiB"`, `"512KiB"`) using
the existing `humanizeutil.ParseBytes` utility. The `MaxBytes` field
type is changed from `int32` to `jsonByteSize`, which is still
backed by `int32` so all existing integer-based configurations
continue to work without changes.

Resolves: #166926
Epic: none

Release note (general change): The `Flush.MaxBytes` field in
`kafka_sink_config` now accepts human-readable byte size strings
(e.g. `"1MiB"`, `"512KiB"`) in addition to raw integer values.
